div.div: AuraKit is framework-agnostic. The Scout agent reads your package.json, tsconfig, Prisma schema, and config files to auto-detect your stack — Next.js, React, Vue, Svelte, Express, FastAPI, Django, and more.
Does AuraKit work with existing projects?
Yes. AuraKit scans your existing codebase first, then generates code that matches your conventions, styling, and architecture. It does not overwrite existing files without confirmation.
What if I already have hooks configured?
The init.sh installer merges AuraKit hooks into your existing settings.json without overwriting. Each hook event is appended — your current hooks stay intact.
Can I use AuraKit on Windows?
Yes. The hooks use bash scripts, so on Windows you need WSL or Git Bash. Claude Code on Windows typically runs through WSL already, so it just works.
How does the compact defense work?
AuraKit sets compact to trigger at 65% token usage. Before compaction, a PreCompact hook saves your work state to disk. After compaction, a PostCompact hook reloads it. Result: zero memory loss.
No. AuraKit is a local skill. Everything runs inside your Claude Code session. No external APIs, no telemetry, no data collection of any kind.
